title: The Three Little Kittens
content:
There were three little kittens
    Put on their mittens
    To eat some Christmas pie.
    Mew, mew,
    Mew, mew,
    Mew, mew, mew.

    These three little kittens
    They lost their mittens,
    And all began to cry.
    Mew, mew, &c.

    "Go, go, naughty kittens,
    And find your mittens,
    Or you shan't have any pie."
    Mew, mew, &c.

    These three little kittens
    They found their mittens,
    And joyfully they did cry.
    Mew, mew, &c.

    "O Granny, dear!
    Our mittens are here,
    Make haste and cut up the pie!"
    Purr-rr, purr-rr, purr-rr-rr.
meaning:
Walter Crane's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Three Little Kittens"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
